message:
fix(web): improve haptics activation check and welcome modal skip button UX

## haptics.ts
- Add `hasUserActivation()` guard that checks navigator.userActivation API
  before attempting to call navigator.vibrate()
- Modern Chromium browsers require a user gesture before allowing vibration,
  and calling vibrate() before any interaction produces noisy console errors
  like "Blocked call to navigator.vibrate because user hasn't tapped on the
  frame yet"
- The userActivation API (navigator.userActivation.hasBeenActive) provides
  a reliable way to detect if the user has interacted with the page
- Falls back to allowing haptics if the browser doesn't expose userActivation,
  maintaining compatibility with older browsers

This eliminates spurious console errors on first page load when the app
attempts to trigger haptic feedback before the user has interacted.

## WelcomeModal.css
- Change skip button text color from --color-text-muted to --color-text-dim
  for better visibility while still being subordinate to the primary CTA
- Add font-weight: var(--font-medium) for improved tap target recognition
- Add text-decoration: underline with 3px offset on hover to provide clear
  affordance that it's an interactive element
- These changes improve accessibility by making the skip option more
  discoverable without competing with the primary onboarding flow
